example_prompt: Send bulk SMS using Africa's Talking new bulk endpoint (JSON-friendly, requires senderId).

phoneNumbers.description: Comma-separated E.164 phone numbers. NOTE: this field is phoneNumbers, not to — using to silently fails.

senderId.description: Your registered sender ID (shortcode or alphanumeric). Required for this endpoint — unlike the legacy send_sms endpoint where from was optional.

enqueue.description: Set to 1 to queue the message without waiting for telco acknowledgement (default).

maskedNumber.description: Hashed phone number for Safaricom Kenya. Use instead of phoneNumbers when sending to a masked subscriber. When set, phoneNumbers must be an empty array [].

telco.description: Service provider name when using maskedNumber. Example: 'Safaricom'. Required when maskedNumber is set.

Message.description: Summary message (e.g. "Sent to 1/1 Total Cost: KES 0.8000").

number.description: Recipient phone number in E.164 format.

messageId.description: Unique message ID assigned by Africa's Talking.

error_schema.description: HTTP 4xx responses return a plain error message string.

gotchas: The recipient field is phoneNumbers (not to). Using to silently fails — this differs from the legacy send_sms endpoint.
senderId is required for this endpoint, not optional. Omitting it returns an error. In the legacy endpoint (send_sms), the from field was optional.
The sandbox URL is coming soon — this endpoint has no sandbox support yet. Use send_sms (legacy) for sandbox testing in the meantime.
This endpoint accepts both application/x-www-form-urlencoded and application/json. Set Content-Type: application/json when sending a JSON body.
To send to a Safaricom hashed number, set maskedNumber to the hashed string, telco to 'Safaricom', and phoneNumbers to an empty array []. The senderId field becomes optional in this mode.
